title: Emotions & Feelings

examples.kp: Masaya ku ngeni!
examples.en: I am happy today!

examples.kp: Masaya ing anak king aldong iti.
examples.en: The child is happy on this day.

entries.notes: Widely attested in Kapampangan greetings (e.g., 'Masayang pasku keka' = Merry Christmas). Shared with Tagalog.

examples.kp: Malungkut ing anak uling wala ne ing asawa na.
examples.en: The child is sad because his spouse is gone.

examples.kp: Malungkut ku nung miras ka.
examples.en: I am lonely when you are away.

entries.notes: Root: lungkut (Wiktionary: Kapampangan noun meaning sadness, loneliness, sorrow, melancholy). Antonym of masaya.

examples.kp: Mimwa ku keka!
examples.en: I am angry at you!

examples.kp: Nung mimwa ka, manghinga ka malugay.
examples.en: When you are angry, breathe slowly.

entries.notes: Attested in tagaloglang.com phrase list: 'Mimwa ku keka' = 'I'm angry at you'. Also appears as 'mimua' in some transliterations.

examples.kp: Matakut kami king bengi.
examples.en: We are afraid at night.

examples.kp: Ali ka matakut; kayabe ku ka.
examples.en: Don't be afraid; I am with you.

entries.notes: Derived from takut (fear, noun; Wiktionary Kapampangan entry). Phrase 'tatakut kami' = 'we are afraid' attested in pdfcoffee Kapampangan phrasebook. Standard ma- adjectival prefix.

examples.kp: Meniglo ku king balita.
examples.en: I am surprised by the news.

examples.kp: Meniglo la ngan king gewa na.
examples.en: They were all astonished at what he did.

entries.notes: Attested on tagaloglang.com and confirmed by Wearkap (Facebook): 'meniglo does not have a direct translation into English'. Used in Kapampangan Bible translation for astonishment (Luke 4:32).

examples.kp: Marine ku migsalita king bayung tau.
examples.en: I am shy when talking to new people.

examples.kp: Marine ya uling binura ne ing sulat na.
examples.en: She is ashamed because she erased her letter.

entries.notes: Listed as a feeling adjective in 'The Use of Kapampangan Adjectives' (SlideShare) and in the pdfcoffee Kapampangan phrase document as 'Marine – Shamed'.

examples.kp: Pagmaragul ku king balen ku.
examples.en: I have pride in my hometown.

examples.kp: Dakal a pagmaragul king puso ku para keka.
examples.en: There is great pride in my heart for you.

entries.notes: Attested as 'Pagmaragul ku' = 'I am proud' in the pdfcoffee Kapampangan phrase document and Tumblr learnkapampangan resource. Root: maragul (great/big).

examples.kp: Marirya ya uling dakal a lugud ku para king anak.
examples.en: He is jealous because I have a lot of love for the child.

examples.kp: Ali ka marirya king biyaya na kapatad mu.
examples.en: Don't be jealous of your sibling's blessings.

entries.notes: Attested on kamaru.blogspot: 'marirya – jealous'. Also listed in the pdfcoffee Kapampangan phrase document as 'Maririya – Jealous'.

examples.kp: Matula ku king lugud mu para kaku.
examples.en: I am glad for your love for me.

examples.kp: Matula la ngan keng kasuyuan king aldo na ita.
examples.en: They were all joyful on that happy day.

entries.notes: Attested in Kapampangan hymn 'Matula Sasamban Daka' (Joyful We Adore Thee) and in Kapampangan religious texts. Expresses a deeper, contented joy distinct from the livelier masaya.

examples.kp: Madalumdum ya ing obra na ngeni.
examples.en: His mood is gloomy today.

examples.kp: Madalumdum ku nung miguran.
examples.en: I feel gloomy when it rains.

entries.notes: Verified Wiktionary entry: Kapampangan adjective meaning 'dark; obscure'. Tagaloglang lists it as meaning 'gloomy' (Tagalog: madilim). Formed with ma- prefix + dalumdum.

examples.kp: Dakal a takut ing nasa puso na.
examples.en: There is much fear in his heart.

examples.kp: Balu mu ing takut ku king bengi?
examples.en: Do you know my fear of the night?

entries.notes: Wiktionary Kapampangan entry: takut = 'fear' (noun). From Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *takut. Cognate with Tagalog takot, Malay takut, Indonesian takut.

examples.kp: Sawa na ku king obra a iti.
examples.en: I am already bored with this work.

examples.kp: Sawa na ku king pane-neng a bisita.
examples.en: I am fed up with the constant visits.

entries.notes: Attested in Wiktionary as a Kapampangan/Philippine form from Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *sawəqaq (eat too much; bored). Shared across Philippine languages.

examples.kp: Ali ya mipaindatun uling kasal ne bukas.
examples.en: He is not calm because his wedding is tomorrow.

examples.kp: Mipaindatun ya king lubu-lubu nang pamagmula.
examples.en: She became calm after a long rest.

entries.notes: Attested on kamaru.blogspot: 'mipaindatun – to become peaceful; to kill restlessness'. The negated form 'ali ya mipaindatun' = 'he is restless' is given as the usage example.

examples.kp: Mataranta ya nung dakal a obra na.
examples.en: He gets flustered when he has a lot of work.

examples.kp: Mataranta ku namu nung ali ku makita ing anak.
examples.en: I get worried when I cannot see the child.

entries.notes: Listed in tagaloglang Kapampangan dictionary. Shared across Philippine languages meaning to be confused, flustered, panicked, or worried. Covers both the worried and flustered emotional states.

examples.kp: Mabaya ku king pusu ku.
examples.en: My heart is aching.

examples.kp: Mabaya ya namu king sinalita mu kaya.
examples.en: She is hurting because of what you said to her.

entries.notes: Attested on kamaru.blogspot Kapampangan word list: 'mabaya – aching'. Covers emotional hurt as well as physical aching; distinct from masakit (sick/painful in a medical sense).

lessons.title: Happy & Sad
lessons.intro: Learn the two most fundamental emotions in Kapampangan — masaya (happy) and malungkut (sad) — plus fear (takut) and the act of crying (kumyak).

accepted: i am happy today!
i am happy today
i'm happy today!
i'm happy today
i am joyful today!
i am joyful today
i'm joyful today!
i'm joyful today

exercises.prompt: Kumyak ya ing anak nung malungkut ya.
accepted: the child cries when he is sad.
the child cries when he is sad
the child weeps when he is sad.
the child weeps when he is sad
the child cries when he is lonely.
the child cries when he is lonely
the child weeps when he is lonely.
the child weeps when he is lonely
the child cries when he is sorrowful.
the child cries when he is sorrowful
the child weeps when he is sorrowful.
the child weeps when he is sorrowful

exercises.prompt: Dakal a ____ ing nasa puso na. (fear)

lessons.title: Anger & Surprise
lessons.intro: Explore two powerful reactive emotions: mimwa (angry) and meniglo (surprised/astonished). These are among the most commonly used emotion words in everyday Kapampangan.

accepted: i am angry at you!
i am angry at you
i'm angry at you!
i'm angry at you
i am mad at you!
i am mad at you
i'm mad at you!
i'm mad at you
i am surprised by the news.
i am surprised by the news
i was surprised by the news.
i was surprised by the news
i am astonished by the news.
i am astonished by the news
i was astonished by the news.
i was astonished by the news
i am amazed by the news.
i am amazed by the news
i was amazed by the news.
i was amazed by the news

exercises.prompt: ____ ya nung dakal a obra na. (flustered/panicked)

lessons.title: Shy & Proud
lessons.intro: Social emotions shape how we relate to others. Learn marine (shy/ashamed), pagmaragul (pride), and marirya (jealous) — three feelings central to Kapampangan social life.

exercises.prompt: Marine ku migsalita king bayung tau.
accepted: i am shy when talking to new people.
i am shy when talking to new people
i'm shy when talking to new people.
i'm shy when talking to new people
i am ashamed when talking to new people.
i am ashamed when talking to new people
i'm ashamed when talking to new people.
i'm ashamed when talking to new people
i am embarrassed when talking to new people.
i am embarrassed when talking to new people
i'm embarrassed when talking to new people.
i'm embarrassed when talking to new people
i have pride in my hometown.
i have pride in my hometown
i am proud of my hometown.
i am proud of my hometown
i have a feeling of pride in my hometown.
i have a feeling of pride in my hometown

exercises.prompt: ____ ya uling dakal a lugud ku para king anak. (jealous)
Dakal a ____ king puso ku para keka. (pride)

lessons.title: Joy & Gloom
lessons.intro: Beyond basic happiness and sadness lie deeper emotional states. Matula expresses a quiet, content joy, while madalumdum captures a dark or gloomy mood. Mabaya describes the ache of emotional pain.

exercises.prompt: Matula ku king lugud mu para kaku.
accepted: i am glad for your love for me.
i am glad for your love for me
i am joyful for your love for me.
i am joyful for your love for me
i am content for your love for me.
i am content for your love for me
my heart is aching.
my heart is aching
my heart aches.
my heart aches
my heart is hurting.
my heart is hurting
my heart is pained.
my heart is pained

exercises.prompt: ____ ya namu king sinalita mu kaya. (hurting/aching)

lessons.title: Bored & Calm
lessons.intro: Sometimes we feel fed up or restless; other times we find peace. Learn sawa (bored/fed up) and mipaindatun (to become calm/peaceful), two contrasting states of inner stillness.

exercises.prompt: Sawa na ku king obra a iti.
accepted: i am already bored with this work.
i am already bored with this work
i am fed up with this work.
i am fed up with this work
i am already fed up with this work.
i am already fed up with this work
i am already weary of this work.
i am already weary of this work

exercises.prompt: Ali ya mipaindatun uling kasal ne bukas.
accepted: he is not calm because his wedding is tomorrow.
he is not calm because his wedding is tomorrow
he is restless because his wedding is tomorrow.
he is restless because his wedding is tomorrow

exercises.prompt: ____ na ku king pane-neng a bisita. (fed up/bored)
____ ya king lubu-lubu nang pamagmula. (became calm)

lessons.title: Emotions Review
lessons.theme: Comprehensive review of all emotion words
lessons.intro: Put it all together! This final lesson reviews all sixteen emotion words from the pack with a mix of translation, fill-in, and matching exercises.

accepted: we are afraid at night.
we are afraid at night
we are scared at night.
we are scared at night
we are frightened at night.
we are frightened at night

exercises.prompt: Ali ka ____ king biyaya na kapatad mu. (jealous)
Sawa na ku king obra a iti.
accepted: i am already bored with this work.
i am already bored with this work
i am fed up with this work.
i am fed up with this work
i am already fed up with this work.
i am already fed up with this work
i am already weary of this work.
i am already weary of this work

sources: https://www.tagaloglang.com/kapampangan-words/ — lists meniglo (surprised), malungkut (sad), masaya (happy), mimwa (angry), marine (shy)
https://www.tagaloglang.com/kapampangan-phrases/ — attests 'Mimwa ku keka' (I am angry at you), masaya in 'Masaya kung akakilala ra ka'
https://www.tagaloglang.com/kapampangan-dictionary/ — lists malungkut, mataranta, kumyak, masaya, among others

https://pdfcoffee.com/common-kapampangan-words-amp-phrasesdocx-pdf-free.html — attests Masaya (Happy), Malungkut (Sad), Mimua (Angry), Marine (Shamed), Maririya (Jealous), Pagmaragul ku (I am proud), Tatakut kami (We are afraid)
https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/lungkut — Kapampangan noun lungkut = sadness, loneliness, sorrow, melancholy
https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/sawa — Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *sawəqaq; Kapampangan sawa = bored, fed up
https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Kapampangan_phrasebook — attests masaya in greeting 'Masaya kung akakilala ra ka'
http://kamaru.blogspot.com/2007/06/random-familiar-kapampangan-words.html — attests marirya (jealous), mabaya (aching), mipaindatun (to become peaceful)
https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/the-use-of-kapampangan-adjectives/39885221 — lists Masaya (Happy), Mimwa (Angry), Marine (Shy), Malungkut (Sad) as Kapampangan feeling adjectives
https://www.facebook.com/wearkapampangan/ — 'meniglo does not have a direct translation into English'; confirms astonishment meaning
https://kaplistorian.blogspot.com — Kapampangan language resource confirming various vocabulary
https://www.sunstar.com.ph — Kapampangan cultural articles confirming matula (joyful) usage in hymn
confidenceNotes: HIGH confidence (multiple independent sources): masaya, malungkut, mimwa, marine, marirya, pagmaragul, meniglo, takut, madalumdum. MEDIUM-HIGH confidence (1-2 attested sources with consistent usage): matakut (derived from attested noun takut with standard ma- prefix; phrase 'tatakut kami' attested), matula (attested in Kapampangan hymn translation), sawa (Wiktionary Proto-Malayo-Polynesian root; Kapampangan usage implied), mipaindatun (attested on kamaru.blogspot with example sentence), mabaya (attested on kamaru.blogspot), kumyak (attested in tagaloglang.com dictionary), mataranta (attested in tagaloglang dictionary; shared Philippine word). Words in AVOID-LIST were not used as headwords. The words lugud, kaluguran, mapagal, and masakit are excluded as headwords per instructions but may appear in example sentences.
